current_fact.fact_text: In 2014, the city of Flint, Michigan switched its drinking water source to the Flint River, inadvertently exposing residents to corrosive water that leached lead from old pipes. The resulting Flint water crisis became a global symbol of environmental injustice, infrastructure neglect, and the human cost of invisible contaminants.

episode.title: Morning Drops #54 - Intentional Listening
episode.description:
Do you remember what it felt like to listen to music without a streaming platform?
In this Morning Drops episode, Gaia reflects on a recent experiment: stepping away from algorithmic listening and going back to an iPod. 

What starts as a small personal choice opens into a wider meditation on attention, presence, and what happens when music is no longer treated as something infinite, but as something we choose with care.

Tracklist:
Scientist - Drum Song Dub
The Other People Place - Let Me Be Me
A Forest Mighty Black - Fresh In My Mind
Cymande - Bra

current_fact.match_reason: A crisis caused by what people couldn't see or taste mirrors an episode about paying close, intentional attention to what flows into our lives—in this case, water vs. sound.

queue.fact_text: On April 21, 2019, a coordinated series of suicide bombings struck churches and hotels across Sri Lanka on Easter Sunday, killing at least 269 people. The attacks targeted both worshippers and tourists, shattering a fragile post-war sense of safety on a major religious holiday.

episode.title: Morning Drops #40 - Diving With Orcas
episode.description: Yasmin's friend Caroline takes us beyond the polar circle, into the Arctic waters of Norway, where orcas and humpback whales gather each winter. A personal journey shaped by a quiet obsession with orcas, from Patagonia to the icy fjords of the north. The soundscape moves gently between cinematic swells and soft, rhythmic pulses — echoing the cold light of Arctic mornings and the grace of underwater encounters.

queue.match_reason: The depth of collective grief after a mass tragedy calls for a reflective, cinematic episode that dwells in quiet waters and the fragile beauty of life.

queue.fact_text: In 1987, a massive car bomb in Colombo, Sri Lanka, attributed to the Liberation Tigers of Tamil Eelam, killed 106 people in a single explosion. It was one of the deadliest attacks in the country’s long civil conflict and highlighted how urban streets can abruptly become war zones.

episode.title: Morning Drops #49 - Rage Against The Machine
episode.description:
In this Morning Drop, Yasmin revisits the story of Rage Against the Machine — a band born in tension, shaped by conviction, and remembered for moments that blurred the line between stage and protest.
From early Los Angeles unrest to Wall Street performances, this episode moves through rupture, resistance, and legacy — carried by songs that still feel urgent today.
Listen closely.
songs: Rage Against the Machine - Killing in the Name
Rage Against the Machine - Without a Face
Rage Against the Machine - Guerrilla Radio
Rage Against the Machine - Sleep Now in the Fire

queue.match_reason: A politically motivated bombing during a civil conflict aligns with an episode about music as protest and the sound of resistance against violence and power.

queue.fact_text: On April 21, 1989, around 100,000 students gathered in Beijing’s Tiananmen Square to mourn reform leader Hu Yaobang, sparking weeks of pro-democracy demonstrations. What began as a memorial turned into one of the most significant protest movements in modern Chinese history.

episode.title: Morning Drops #08 - (un)Boxing
episode.description: An episode about breaking free from expectations and labels. Music that helps us unbox ourselves and embrace who we truly are.
songs: Kendrick Lamar - The Blacker the Berry

queue.match_reason: A vast student movement demanding reform and freedom resonates with an episode about breaking out of boxes and challenging imposed identities.

queue.fact_text: In 1945, as World War II neared its end in Europe, Soviet forces south of Berlin attacked the German military high command at Zossen. Their advance into the heart of Nazi command structures signaled that the Third Reich’s days were numbered.

episode.title: Morning Drops #42 - The Joy Of Learning
episode.description:
Hosted by Yasmin, this Morning Drop explores learning as a lifelong process, focusing on persistence, repetition, and the emotional landscape that comes with challenging oneself over time.

Through personal stories and scientific insights, the episode looks at how learning reshapes the brain, generates joy through effort, and why progress often arrives after confusion and frustration. It reflects on the so-called delayed pleasure effect, the role of dopamine in motivation, and how curiosity and learning support long-term wellbeing.

The episode also questions our cultural relationship with failure and invites a softer, more realistic approach to learning, one that accepts mistakes as part of growth and sees persistence as a quiet form of strength.

Mood: reflective, grounded, quietly hopeful
Music genre: alternative rock, hip hop, electronic, neo-soul, drill

A space to listen slowly and stay with the process.

queue.match_reason: The slow, grinding advance that finally breaks a war machine can be read as a harsh lesson in persistence and the high cost of learning from history, fitting a reflective episode about perseverance and growth.

queue.fact_text: On April 21, 1806, during the Napoleonic Wars, a French frigate under Captain Bernard Dubourdieu fought off a superior British squadron in the Action of 21 April 1806 off South Africa. The French ship escaped despite heavy damage, becoming a small but celebrated tale of naval audacity.

episode.title: Morning Drops #22 - Sicilians On The Dolomites
episode.description: Hosted by Yasmin, a musical journey inspired by the mountains. Joined by two special guests, exploring the tracks they've chosen, hearing the stories behind their picks, and discovering the personal memories each song brings to life — a blend of music, voices, and landscapes you can hear.
songs: Florian Pellissier Quintet - Coup de foudre à Thessalonique
Lucio Dalla & Francesco De Gregori - Ma come fanno i marinai

queue.match_reason: A daring naval escape by sailors (“marinai”) pairs neatly with an episode whose tracklist explicitly includes a song about sailors and sea-going lives.

queue.fact_text: In 1809, on the first day of the Battle of Eckmühl, Napoleon’s forces drove two Austrian corps from the town of Landshut while holding off the main Austrian army to the north. The maneuver showcased his talent for dividing opponents and striking quickly at vulnerable points.

episode.title: Sedna FM #06 - Alters of an Ego
episode.description: What if you could step outside yourself and become someone freer, bolder, or completely unexpected? Exploring the artistry of musicians who craft alter-egos and parallel realities to experiment with new dimensions of their identity. It's not about hiding who they are — it's about expanding who they can be.
songs: The Fireman - Strawberries Oceans Ships Forest

queue.match_reason: Napoleon’s mythic persona and strategic theater of war echoes an episode about alter-egos, identity performance, and the art of crafting larger-than-life roles.

queue.fact_text: Back in 1796, during the Montenotte Campaign, Napoleon decisively defeated Piedmontese forces at the Battle of Mondovì, forcing Piedmont’s surrender within a week. The victory flipped the Italian campaign in France’s favor and helped cement his reputation as a rising military genius.

episode.title: Sedna FM #01 - November Bomb
episode.description: The first Family & Friends radio episode from Yasmin Sarbaoui and Gaia Parolini. Where it all began — a life soundtrack that launched Sedna FM into the universe. Raw, heartfelt, and full of the energy that defines everything that followed.
songs: Canserbero - De la vida como pelicula, tragedia, comedia y ficcion

queue.match_reason: A decisive early victory that launched Napoleon’s legend parallels Sedna’s explosive first episode—the beginning of a campaign, but in sound instead of battles.

queue.fact_text: On April 21, 43 BC, the Battle of Mutina saw Mark Antony defeated again by consular forces led by Aulus Hirtius, who died in the fighting. Though Antony failed to take Mutina, his rival Decimus Brutus was soon murdered, plunging the Roman Republic deeper into its power struggles.

episode.title: Morning Drops #44 - Residente
episode.description:
In this edition of Morning Drops, Yasmin navigates the complex geography of René Pérez Joglar, known to the world as Residente. His story is not just a musical evolution; it is a timeline marked by resistance, lyrical obsession, and a relentless search for identity.

From the gated streets of Trujillo Alto to the global stage, we explore how a restless mind transformed into the voice of a generation. The selection moves through the textures of his life: hearing the raw energy of his early rebellion, the sharp precision of his political critique, and finally, the fragile silence of his most personal confessions.

It is a study in duality. We witness the rapper who dismantles giants and the son who misses his mother. It is a soundscape that proves that sometimes, the loudest revolution happens inside.

Mood: Raw, Revolutionary, Introspective, Honest
Music genre: Latin Alternative, Hip Hop, Urban, World

queue.match_reason: Ancient Roman power struggles and assassinations mirror the political critique and inner revolutions explored through Residente’s socially charged lyrics.

queue.fact_text: On April 21, 180 BC’s successor centuries later, but on this date in 1946 and 1967 storms struck; yet much earlier, by April 21 in the Roman calendar, the city of Rome itself was traditionally said to have been founded. However, this same date in 43 BC is marked by the bloody Battle of Mutina, reminding Romans that the city’s mythic origins coexisted with very real civil wars.

episode.title: Sedna FM #04 - Queen of Spades
episode.description: Tuning in from the planet of irrational love. Exploring love's most vulnerable side — the kind that awakens the child within us. A diverse music selection ranging from electric blues to downtempo, soul, and jazz. A tribute to the feeling that has moved artists to create the most beautiful works. Love makes us vulnerable, and there is no art without vulnerability.
songs: Alabama Shakes - Gimme All Your Love
Queens of the stone age - Make it wit chu
Etta James - I'd Rather Go Blind
Eric Clapton - Have you ever loved a woman

queue.match_reason: Mark Antony’s legendary romances and tragic political choices echo the episode’s themes of irrational love, vulnerability, and how passion shapes destiny.

queue.fact_text: On April 21, 2010, as the Kharkiv Pact was signed, it also underscored how energy, borders, and identity are entangled in Eastern Europe. For many Ukrainians, the deal symbolized both economic pragmatism and a contested narrative about whose stories would define their future.

episode.title: Morning Drops #20 - Hitchhiking Poland
episode.description: Yasmin takes you back to the summer of 2020, when borders were still uncertain and a long-held wish to visit Poland finally came true. Through four tracks, tracing her journey from a stranger's couch in Poznań to the peaks of Zakopane.

queue.match_reason: An episode centered on traveling through Poland and Eastern Europe’s borderlands pairs well with a treaty that reshaped regional dynamics just across the border in Ukraine.

queue.fact_text: On April 21, 1964–1965’s World’s Fair reopened and decades later on the same date, Flint changed its water source; but this day is also remembered for colossal tornadoes and failed satellites. Each April 21 entry shows how a single calendar day can hold both utopian visions of the future and sober lessons about risk and responsibility.

episode.title: Morning Drops #14 - Slow Planet
episode.description: A pause. A quiet return to slower rhythms, sparked by a live set under the summer sky. A moment to breathe and settle into the week.

queue.match_reason: A slow, contemplative episode offers a planetary pause to reflect on how one date can carry both dazzling fairs and painful environmental mistakes.

queue.fact_text: Taken together, the events of April 21 span Moon landings, ancient battles, tornadoes, treaties, protests, musicals, and environmental crises. It’s a reminder that history isn’t a single story but a mosaic of human curiosity, conflict, creativity, and the forces of nature that shape us.

episode.title: Evening Flows #00 - Introduction to evening flows
episode.description:
Sedna FM introduces a new format: Evening Flow, where you can enjoy curated music sets from selectors, DJs, and artists.

Each mix is an invitation to drift beyond the expected. A space where hidden gems from personal archives come to light, where sound speaks its own language. There are no rules here, no patterns to follow. Just the steady unfolding of a mood, a texture, a journey shaped by feeling.

Let the music lead the way - enjoy the trip!

queue.match_reason: A day that contains multitudes deserves an open‑ended, rule‑free mix, inviting listeners to drift across the full spectrum of April 21’s stories.

published.fact_text: In 1972, Apollo 16 astronauts John Young and Charles Duke piloted the lunar module Orion to the Moon's Descartes Highlands, achieving the fifth crewed landing on the lunar surface. Over three moonwalks, they drove more than 26 kilometers in the lunar rover and collected over 90 kilograms of rocks to unravel the Moon's volcanic history.

episode.title: Morning Drops #03 - Space Shift
episode.description: Gaia invites you to shift your mindset with a simple yet powerful reflection: What if we saw emptiness not as a void but as space to create? An energising and feel-good selection of tracks, the perfect way to ease into your day and set the tone for the week ahead.
songs: Willy J Healey - Woke Up Smiling
The Strokes - You Only Live Once

published.match_reason: A literal leap into space pairs naturally with a "Space Shift" episode about rethinking emptiness as creative potential.

published.fact_text: On April 21, 1964, the navigation satellite Transit-5BN-3 failed to reach orbit and broke up on reentry, scattering about 0.95 kg of plutonium from its nuclear power source into the upper atmosphere. It was one of the earliest large-scale tests of how a high-altitude release of radioactive material disperses globally.

episode.title: Morning Drops #27 - Nomophobia
episode.description: Yasmin reflects on what happened when she recently lost her phone and why it felt freeing. A dive into the psychology of our relationship with smartphones: from nomophobia (the fear of being without a phone), to digital burnout and cognitive overload, to the surprising benefits of boredom and the brain's default mode network. An invitation to rethink how we connect and disconnect.

published.match_reason: A failed navigation satellite and dispersed plutonium echo the hidden infrastructure and risks behind our seamless tech lives, matching an episode about rethinking our relationship to technology.

published.fact_text: In 1946, a tornado that struck near Timber Lake, South Dakota, was recorded as roughly 4 miles (6.4 km) wide, making it among the widest tornadoes ever documented at the time. Its immense breadth showed how some tornadoes can become sprawling atmospheric machines instead of thin, twisting ropes.

episode.title: Morning Drops #45 - It Can't Rain Forever
episode.description:
In this first broadcast of 2026, Gaia returns from the vivid landscapes of Morocco to open a space for reflection. This episode navigates the delicate transition between closing a significant chapter and stepping into the new year. It explores the concept of constancy amidst change, celebrating the grounding force of friendship and the creative sanctuary that is Sedna FM.

The musical selection mirrors this journey, moving from the orchestral depth of changing seasons into the golden warmth of psychedelic soul. It recalls memories of Swiss mountains and unexpected downpours, reminding us that shared optimism can weather any storm.

Take a moment to breathe in the new morning and listen deeply.

Mood: Reflective, Warm, Transitional, Hopeful
Music genre: Classic Rock, Psychedelic Soul, Pop Rock, Soft Rock

published.match_reason: A colossal tornado fits with a weather-themed episode about storms, rain, and finding hope after turbulent skies.

published.fact_text: On April 21, 1967, a violent tornado outbreak tore across Illinois, killing more than 50 people and injuring over 1,000. The town of Belvidere was especially hard hit when a tornado struck just as students were being dismissed from school, turning a routine afternoon into a historic tragedy.

episode.title: Morning Drops #05 - Electric Shock
episode.description: An energizing episode with electrifying sounds to wake you up and get you moving. High energy tracks that deliver a jolt of musical electricity to start your day.

published.match_reason: The sudden, destructive energy of a tornado outbreak parallels the raw, electrified charge of an "Electric Shock" rock set.

published.fact_text: On April 21, 1965, the 1964–1965 New York World's Fair opened for its second and final season, showcasing futuristic visions of technology, space travel, and global culture. Visitors could see early prototypes of picturephones, marvel at the massive Unisphere, and sample a corporate dream of the world to come.

episode.title: Sedna FM #02 - Destination Sedna
episode.description: Continuing the Sedna FM journey together with family and friends! Episode two of the main series — exploring new musical territories and deepening the connection with our listeners. A collective voyage through sound.
songs: Odion Iruoje - Identify With Your Roots
Depeche mode - world in my eyes (Oil Tank Mix)

published.match_reason: A world's fair of futuristic exhibits and global pavilions matches an episode framed as a collective voyage through diverse musical territories.

published.fact_text: In 1958, United Air Lines Flight 736 collided mid-air with a U.S. Air Force fighter jet over Nevada, killing all 49 people aboard both aircraft. The disaster became a catalyst for stricter airspace management and radar control around busy flight corridors.

episode.title: Morning Drops #31 - Sailing With Germans
episode.description: From the deck of a sailing boat, hosted by Yasmin and her special guests. A week at sea with six Germans, each with their own stories, songs, and ways of moving through the waves. From shared meals to unexpected laughter, from quiet resilience to sun-soaked joy — a collection of voices and tracks that reflect what it means to journey together.
songs: Ben Howard - Keep Your Head Up

published.match_reason: A tragic transport accident that reshaped safety contrasts with a gentle journey-at-sea episode, both reflecting on how we move through space and depend on shared rules to travel safely.

published.fact_text: On April 21, 2021, the Indonesian submarine KRI Nanggala (402) disappeared during a torpedo drill in the Bali Sea and was later found broken into pieces at a depth of over 800 meters. All 53 sailors on board were lost, turning a routine exercise into one of Indonesia’s most haunting naval disasters.

episode.title: Morning Drops #33 - Immerse
episode.description: An analogy between diving, one of Gaia's great passions, and the act of immersing in life itself. A reflection on what happens when we go below the surface: when we let ourselves be fully present. From moments of calm suspension to warm, rhythmic flow, the selection moves like water — fluid, organic, and alive. An invitation to slow down, listen deeper, and float.
songs: Pino D'Angiò - Un Po' d'Uva e un Liquore

published.match_reason: A submarine lost in the depths of the sea resonates poignantly with an episode built around immersion, water, and going below the surface.

published.fact_text: In 1982, Milwaukee Brewers pitcher Rollie Fingers became the first pitcher in Major League Baseball history to record 300 career saves. His handlebar moustache and late-inning dominance helped define the modern idea of a "closer"—a specialist trusted to protect narrow leads.

episode.title: Sedna FM #05 - On The Go - Zero Zero
episode.description: An energetic mix for when you're on the move. Zero Zero captures that feeling of starting fresh, moving forward, and embracing the journey. Perfect for travel, walks, or just getting things done.
songs: Grace Jones - Pull up to the bumper
Easy star all star - Soul love
James Brown - Talking loud and saying nothing (remix)

published.match_reason: The idea of a relentless closer who finishes games aligns with a kinetic, forward-driving episode designed for movement and getting things done.

published.fact_text: On April 21, 1977, the musical "Annie" opened on Broadway, turning a Depression-era comic strip about an optimistic orphan into a global theatre phenomenon. Its songs, especially "Tomorrow," became cultural touchstones about resilience and hope in hard times.

episode.title: Morning Drops #01 - Timeless Legacy
episode.description: Gaia guides us through timeless music to start the morning with the right foot! The first episode of the Morning Drops series — classic sounds that never fade.
songs: Roberta Flack - Killing me softly with his song

published.match_reason: A beloved Broadway musical with enduring songs fits with an episode about timeless tracks and legacies that never fade.

published.fact_text: In 1948, the United Nations Security Council adopted Resolution 47 on the dispute over Kashmir, calling for a ceasefire, troop withdrawals, and a plebiscite to determine the region's future. The vote sketched a blueprint for peace that, decades later, remains only partially fulfilled.

episode.title: Morning Drops #39 - Moving Meditation
episode.description: An episode inspired by how travel helps us see more clearly. Stepping into new places opens the mind, shifts perspective. The music moves through balearic warmth, boogie grooves, world and latin-folk influences, and touches of new wave — a selection built to follow the quiet changes that happen when you're out of your usual rhythm.
songs: España Circo Este - Dormo poco e sogno molto
Talking Heads - This Must Be the Place (Naive Melody)

published.match_reason: A long, unresolved border conflict and attempts at mediation align with an episode about travel, perspective shifts, and moving meditations on how we relate to place.

published.fact_text: On April 21, 2010, Ukraine and Russia signed the Kharkiv Pact, extending Russia’s lease on the Sevastopol naval base in Crimea in exchange for discounted natural gas. The agreement temporarily deepened ties while foreshadowing the geopolitical tensions that would erupt just a few years later.

episode.title: Morning Drops #52- Think Again
episode.description:
In this Morning Drop, Yasmin explores the quiet art of changing your mind, guided by Adam Grant's book Think Again. From the beliefs we carry without questioning to the identities we build around what we think we know, this episode follows the slow, sometimes uncomfortable process of unlearning.

Along the way, we meet a Serbian trumpet player who had to let go of one dream to find another, a Tunisian oud master who challenged centuries of tradition from the inside, and a Japanese band who found freedom in playing for no one. The music moves from Balkan brass to Mediterranean stillness to psychedelic drift, tracing the shape of a mind opening up.

Listen slowly, and ask yourself: is there something you believe today that deserves a second look?

Tracklist:
Boban Marković Orkestar – SAT
Anouar Brahem – The Astounding Eyes of Rita
Kikagaku Moyo – Dripping Sun
Son Lux – Lost It to Trying

published.match_reason: A controversial treaty about strategic interests invites reconsideration and hindsight, matching an episode explicitly about rethinking assumptions and unlearning.

published.fact_text: In 2014, the city of Flint, Michigan switched its drinking water source to the Flint River, inadvertently exposing residents to corrosive water that leached lead from old pipes. The resulting Flint water crisis became a global symbol of environmental injustice, infrastructure neglect, and the human cost of invisible contaminants.
